Get started
Communicate
on
your
terms
.
Sovereign, interoperable and secure communications, built on the Matrix open standard.
Get started
Download
Own your data
.
Choose your hosting
.
Secure your comms
.
Trusted by secure organisations
Be in your element
.
We built Element on the Matrix open standard to give users the independence to control and manage their communications.
Sovereignty
.
Ownership
.
Control
.
Digital sovereignty
Free from proprietary, vendor-locked platforms
.
Element gives each and every government department the ability to run and control their own digitally sovereign, interoperable and secure communications solution.
“We are working together towards one goal: European digital sovereignty.”
Friedrich Merz, Chancellor of Germany
DeCentralised
Centralised
Centralised
Decentralisation puts you in control
Built on the decentralised Matrix open standard, to free you from vendor-locked systems.
Digital commons
Sovereign cross-border collaboration
.
Element sits at the heart of Europe’s digitally sovereign and open source office productivity suites.
Digital sovereignty means not being reliant on a specific vendor. That’s why we build on the interoperable Matrix open standard, and support a competitive ecosystem.
"
Digital sovereignty, i.e. control, design and interchangeability in digital systems, cannot be achieved without open source software.
"
Open Source Business Alliance
Open standard interoperability
Communicate between organisations and nations
.
Make decisions faster and work more efficiently  across governments and public institutions.
Bob
@bob:matrix.org
Alice
@alice:element.io
Connectivity between organisations
Matrix offers vendor agnostic federation to enable different solutions from different vendors to work together. Think of the Matrix open standard as SMTP for real time communications.
Independence from siloed systems
The transparency of open source software, and building on the interoperable
Matrix open standard
means Element’s customers are never vendor-locked.
“Digital sovereignty means having choices, so no single technology and provider become a dependency that can be used against our interests. It is always good to have choices in order to avoid dependencies.”
Karsten Wildberger, federal minister for Bundesministerium für Digitales und Staatsmodernisierung
Sovereign collaboration
Alternative to Microsoft Teams
.
Replace vendor-locked productivity suites with self-hosted workplace collaboration for desktop and mobile.
Based on the Matrix open standard, protect sensitive information while enabling real-time communication with partner organisations.
Sovereign messenger
WhatsApp and Signal replacement
.
A consumer-style messaging app to ease adoption and encourage use.
End-to-end encrypted messaging and calls, connected to workplace systems and built for public sector compliance requirements.
77%
of IT leaders say that their organisation struggles to maintain privacy and control of data shared via communications technologies.
Source: A commissioned study conducted by Forrester Consulting on behalf of Element
Air-gapped networks
Collaboration in isolation
.
Element offers install and support for
air-gapped
, isolated and high-side instances with no internet connectivity needed.
64%
expect their organisation’s need to give partners access  to air-gapped or isolated environments to grow over the next 2 to 3 years.
Source: A commissioned study conducted by Forrester Consulting on behalf of Element
Secure by design
Resilient communications
.
Element utilises the decentralised Matrix open standard to create a resilient, zero trust, communications network.
With no central point of failure, Element protects against global outages and ensures high availability through the use of redundant servers.
Element also supports air-gapped and low bandwidth communications, including Mesh and satellite connectivity.
The professionalised Matrix-based server that organisations can use to host any Matrix-based solution. Deploy sovereign, interoperable and secure real time communications at scale.
Our partners
Creators of Matrix
.
Element is led by the creators of Matrix, the decentralised open standard for secure and sovereign real time communication.
Created by the Element team in 2014.
Governed by The Matrix.org Foundation.
200M+
users worldwide
100K+
deployments
60M+
largest public
deployment
500K+
largest commercial deployment
Be in your element
.
Sovereign, interoperable and secure communications, built on the Matrix open standard.
Get started

Get started
Pricing plans
.
Sovereign and secure communications. Self-hosted, and also available air-gapped, with powerful enterprise performance and functionality.
Community
.
Non-professional deployments and evaluations.
Self-hosted
Element Server Suite Community
Open source under AGPL
Official FOSS distribution from Element
Free of charge frontend and backend
Includes Element Call
Set up a server
Get the free app
Enterprise
.
Professional use, enterprise-grade performance and features.
Self-hosted
Element Server Suite Pro
Includes
Synapse Pro
Enhanced security and support
Corporate oversight and control
Dynamic scaling and cost savings
Talk to an expert
Priced per seat/month
Sovereign
.
Complete sovereignty with no dependence on the internet.
Air-gapped
Element Server Suite Pro
Includes
Synapse Pro
Operate without internet connectivity
Dedicated mobile push gateway
Air-gapped
installation
Talk to an expert
Priced per deployment
Community
Enterprise
Sovereign
Number of users
1-100
From 100
Per deployment
Backend
homeServer
Synapse
Community edition homeserver, actively developed by Element, but not for use in production environments.
Synapse
PRO
Next generation homeserver for professional use that provides dynamic scaling and 90%+ resource savings compared to Community Synapse.
Security enhancements
Advance Security Advisory
Advance notification of higher severity security releases to ensure the deployment is always maximally secure.
Curated full stack security updates
Easy deployment of security updates.
Compliance
Corporate compliance and control
Enterprise features for corporate oversight and management.
Cyber Resilience Act compliance
Software Bill of Materials (SBOMs)
Global accessibility compliance
Accessibilty conformance reports available for clients up to WCAG AA 2.2 & BITV 2.0
ISO/IEC 27001:2022
Element is independently audited and certified as compliant with the ISO/IEC 27001:2022 information security management standard.
ISO/IEC 5230
Element’s products are developed in accordance with ISO/IEC 5230 ("OpenChain"), an international standard for open source license compliance.
Cyber Essentials Plus
Element holds Cyber Essentials Plus, a UK government backed certification scheme for cybersecurity.
Support
Installation support
Setup, configurations and onboarding from the Element team.
Long Term Support (LTS)
LTS versions released every 6 months for guaranteed stability; backports of critical security fixes.
Level 3 support
For all functionality issues and bug fixes with response times based on priority.
Service Level Agreement (SLA)
Guaranteed response times.
4 Business hours
Enhanced SLAs available
Training and Deployment
Enable your teams with expert-led onboarding. One Time training for administrators and trainers.
Administration
Admin interface
Configure and control your deployment from a single pane of glass.
Advanced Identity and Access Management
Integrate with enterprise identity management systems (LDAP and SCIM) to automate user management and mirror organisational structures within room hierarchies.
LDAP / AD authentication
Authenticate users against an external LDAP or Active Directory system.
Single Sign-On
OIDC authentication to support SSO.
Auditing and reporting
Visible in-room auditing to capture an official record of discussion and decision-making for regulation and compliance purposes.
Moderating
Corporate oversight and management of your organisation’s conversations.
Retention policies
Configure rules-based retention policies for messages and media, to meet your organisation's processes and compliance requirements.
Data export
The ability to easily migrate your data, protecting against vendor lock-in.
Internal & External collaboration
Cross domain gateway
Hardware-based solution to enable data sharing between information classification boundaries or domains, such as high-side and low-side environments.
Add on
Secure border gateway
Rules-based application firewall with federation controls to securely manage your Matrix network.
Federation
Enable or block federation.
Deployment
Air-gapped deployment
Install and support for air-gapped, isolated and high-side instances. No internet connectivity needed.
Custom mobile push gateway
Push notifications via your own infrastructure.
Support for OpenShift
Helm charts
Official helm charts from Element. Easy Kubernetes-based installation. Ready to use with GitOps processes and Continuous Deployment (CD) tooling.
Scaling, availability and efficiency
Auto-scaling
Dynamically scale to save resources and ensure a great end user experience.
High availability and rolling updates
Automatic failover and rolling updates for additional stability and to minimise downtime.
S3 support
Support for media repository stored on S3.
Resource savings
Optimise resource usage for cost savings and minimised environmental impact.
Frontend
Workplace app
Element Pro
Enterprise-grade versions of the Element frontend, with server-side control for workplace use.
Whitelabelled mobile app
Fully customised app for mobile and desktop.
Add on
Add on
Custom colour theme
Apply your own brand colour to the app.
Certificate pinning
Utilise TLS certificate pinning to specify and ensure connection with the intended server.
Mobile device management
Automate the connection of your employees’ Element Pro app to your homeserver.
Sovereign analytics and reporting
Receive sensitive analytics and rageshakes internally, rather than routing to a third party provider.
Seamless deployment
Provision via your internal platform, or the usual app stores.
Easier onboarding
Extended organisation-wide chat room management with group and role-based access control.
Enable content scanning
Enhanced content scanning to support antivirus and data loss protection.
Grid View
Monitor, send and receive messages across multiple rooms simultaneously.
Custom domain
Choose your own user addresses based on your brand (for example @user:organisation.com).
Messaging & collaboratioN
Messaging
Threads, read receipts, location sharing, attachments, voice messages, polls, emojis, reactions and much more.
Collaboration
Embedded links and share-to capabilities, file sharing, polls, reactions and more.
Integrations
Widgets and integrations such as whiteboard (NeoBoard) and project management (OpenProject, Jira).
Voice and video calls (Element Call)
E2EE voice and video for 1:1 and group chat.
End-to-end encryption
The entire platform is end-to-end encrypted by default. Configuration can enable the creation of non-encrypted chat rooms.
Cross-signed device verification
Verify a device using QR code or an emoji string
Private rooms
The ability to create invite-only rooms.
Data history
Unlimited data history.
Mobile and desktop
Available on Windows, Mac and Linux. Download from the App store, Google Play, F-Droid or use in browser.
Built on the Matrix Open standard
Own and control your data
Self-hosted communications.
Decentralised
No single point of failure or global outages.
Interoperability
Secure communications between separate organisations with different frontends.
Security
End-to-end encrypted by default. Only those in the conversation can decrypt messages and data, including voice & video.
Open source
Built on open source software to ensure users can trust the system.
Independence
No vendor lock-in, interoperable with any other Matrix-based system.
Protects end user privacy
No personal phone numbers required.
Maximum 1 set up per product (includes only one (1) production server and one (1) test server. Additional servers will be subject to the same price minimums)
Accessibility
.
Element is deeply committed to accessibility, embedding inclusive design across our product set. We align our efforts with WCAG 2.1 Level AA and EN 301 549 criteria, with ongoing audits to support accessibility improvements, assistive-technology compatibility, high-contrast modes and full keyboard navigability.
Read our complete
accessibility statement

Get started
Element help and support
.
Check the FAQs below or send a message in a room to get community support or get in touch with our team.
Desktop & Web
Join room
iOS
Join room
Android
Join room
Documentation
View docs
You should never share account details in any of these rooms. For account specific questions, please email
support@element.io
.
🔗
Account management
🔗
How do I reset my password?
Please note that we cannot reset passwords or reactivate matrix.org accounts. If you have an email address attached to your account, you can reset your password
here
.
🔗
Do I need an email address to register?
Using an email address when creating a Matrix account is optional; it will make it easier for others to discover you and can help you to reset your password if needed.
It's ultimately up to the administrator of the server to decide whether or not you need to use an email address when creating a Matrix account.
For accounts on the matrix.org homeserver, supplying an email address during registration is optional, but matrix.org reserves the right to occasionally require it for abuse mitigation as referred to in the
privacy policy
.
🔗
What is the username used for?
We will use your username to create an ID (Matrix ID) allowing to disambiguate you from others with the same display name. It will allow us to keep your email confidential. 🙂
🔗
Can I register with a phone number?
Not anymore. Sorry!
🔗
Chat
🔗
How can I mention someone?
Just type their name and they will be notified accordingly. You can autocomplete by pressing the tab button, you don’t need any prefix for the mention to work!
🔗
With what type of mentions will I get notified?
By default you’ll be notified if your display name and your user name are mentioned. You can add other names, nicknames or keywords on which you want to be notified from your settings, in the Notifications section.
🔗
How do I send a file?
Simple, just drag the file into Element and it will automatically upload. Alternatively you can click on the paperclip icon in the text input field and browse your filesystem.
🔗
Can I upload a file from a mobile device?
Yes you can! On a mobile device simply press the arrow which is pointing upwards (on iOS) or the + symbol (on android) while in a chat and select your file.
🔗
How can I invite a contact to use Element?
You can invite someone to a room by using the "Invite to this room" button in the right hand side "Room members list" using their Matrix ID (if they have one) or by email. Those without a Matrix ID will be able to preview the room if the room allows for that.
🔗
Is there a way to know if a message has been read?
Yes! Element shows who has read a message by displaying their avatar to the right of the message. Hovering over these avatars (or clicking on them on mobile) will give you the user’s info and reading time.
🔗
How can I search for a file or message?
To search a room from Desktop, click on the magnifying glass located near the top of the screen. You can then type in a keyword or filename that you are searching for with the ability to select whether you want to search within the specific chat room or across all conversations. For encrypted rooms, search only works on Desktop (not Element web and mobile) and you need to have "Message search" enabled in the Element Security & Privacy settings.
🔗
Settings
🔗
How do I change my account settings?
Click on the drop down menu under your name in the top left corner of the web or desktop app and select. From here you can change all of your account and general Element preferences.
🔗
How do I change my notification settings?
Element allows you to customise your notifications at two levels: across the app and per room. You can configure how you will be notified for given events by default in the Notifications section of your Settings (accessible from the drop down menu under your name in the top left corner of the web/desktop app).
You can configure keywords, default notification settings for group, one-to-one chat rooms, invites and calls. The notifications can be:
– Turned off: you won’t be notified when the selected event happens.
– Turned on: you will get a message popping up when the selected event happens, but no sound.
– “Noisy”: you will get a visual highlight (red badge and/or text highlight), a sound and/or vibration (depending on the device) when the selected event happens.
Then for finer tuning, you can easily configure per room notification setting from the context menu you get by clicking on the [...] icon that appears when hovering over the room name in the room list. This is very handy when you wish to temporarily mute a room, or make sure you’re not missing anything from a given discussion. You can select the following options:
– Mute: you won’t be notified, even if your name or a keyword is mentioned.
– Mentions only: you will only be notified for the items that are meant to be ‘noisy’, i.e. your name and keywords.
– All messages: you will get a (silent) notification for every message happening in the room; your ‘noisy’ events (e.g. your name being mentioned) will still be noisy (red badge and sound).
– All messages (noisy): every message will make a noise on top of the visual notification. Your noisy events will still be differentiated by a red badge.
🔗
How do I set up email notifications?
You can set Element up to email you when you have missed some activity (new messages, new invites…). You can do this in the Notification section of your Settings and turn on the toggle labelled as ‘Enable email notifications’.
🔗
How can I change my display name?
You can change your display name in General section of your Settings (accessible from the drop down menu under your name in the top left corner of the web/desktop app).
🔗
How do I reset my password?
The Element team won't be able to reset passwords or reactivate matrix.org accounts.
If you have forgotten your password, please visit
app.element.io
and click on the link ‘Set a new password’. You will need to input the email address which is registered to your Element account and choose a new password. An email will then be sent to confirm your request to reset your password. Please follow the link in the email to complete the process and regain access to your account.
If you haven't set an email address for your Element account, please contact your homeserver administrator who may choose to reset your password for you.
🔗
Why would I need to associate an email address to my account? Can I do so a posteriori?
It's ultimately up to the administrator of the server to decide whether or not you need to use an email address when creating a Matrix account, and optionally restrict this to certain email addresses or domains.
If you can, we strongly recommend that you set an email address in order to be able to reclaim your account if you’ve lost your password. It is also useful for people to find you, and easier to remember than (yet another) ID. If you haven’t done so at registrationdon’t worry; you can add as many email addresses as you like, at any point, in the General section of your Settings
If you have forgotten your password and did not register an email address with your account, your homeserver administrator may choose to reset the password for you. If not, we're sorry to say that there is no other way to access your account. 🙁
🔗
Rooms
🔗
How can I change the settings for a specific chat or room?
You can change the settings for any one-to-one chat or group room by clicking on the cog icon next to the room name.
🔗
Can I restrict the access of a room to a given set of people?
You can restrict the access of a room to people who have been invited by selecting the “Private (invite only)" option in the "Security & Privacy" settings of the room. People knowing the link of the room won’t be able to access it if an invite hasn’t been explicitly sent to their email address or ID.
By adding the room to a Space, you can also select "Space members" to only allow anyone that is a member of the Space to join.
🔗
Can I limit the access of a room to people knowing its existence?
You can restrict the access of a room to people with whom you shared a link to the room by selecting the “anyone who knows the room link” option in the room settings. Selecting this option will not make the room publicly visible to the rest of the community.
🔗
Can I make a room publicly discoverable?
You can get your room listed into your server’s directory by checking the button labeled "Publish this room to the public in example.com's room directory?". By checking this box, it will be discoverable to anyone searching for a room on the server. However, they will be able to join only if you haven’t restricted the access only to people with an invitation.
🔗
Will anyone be able to join my room if I list it in the directory?
If your room is listed in the directory, people will know it exists but they will only be able to join it if you allowed the access to it to anyone knowing the link.
🔗
Can new members read the history of the room?
Yes. If the room history visibility is set to “Members (full history)” in the room’s Privacy & Security settings, new members will be able to read messages that were sent before they were invited.
In rooms that are end-to-end encrypted, the feature is currently under development and will be available soon. When Greg invites Kate to such an end-to-end encrypted room, Kate is seeing a decoration on the past messages that they have been shared by Greg.
As Kate was not in the room when the past messages were sent, it is Greg who provides her with the message decryption keys. Even though most regular users won’t make use of this information in nominal situations, and the messages can still be trusted, it is important from a security perspective to have full transparency on the provenance of the message decryption keys, and the trust anchors. If Greg was malicious and could also find a malicious server admin to cooperate with, they together could manipulate those past messages.
In contrast, if the history visibility is set to “Members since invited”, the new members won’t see the messages that were sent before they were invited.
Note: when changing the history visibility of the room, it won’t affect the visibility of the messages that have been already sent. The setting that was present at the time of sending the message, is what matters. For example, if at the time of sending the message the visibility was “Members since invited”, then this message will be forever visible only to members who were invited (or already members) at the time of sending this message. Hence, when you switch from “Members since invited” to “Members (full history)” on Tuesday at 10, the future members can’t read messages that were sent before Tuesday at 10.
Note: in end-to-end encrypted rooms, history is only available when the new member is explicitly invited to the room. If the room allows members of a space to join without an invite, and the new member uses that option to join, they won’t be able to see history.
🔗
If I make the room accessible to anyone, will new members be able to read the history?
No. Changing the room’s access (e.g. who can join the room) will not automatically affect the history visibility of the room (e.g. who can see history). For example, if the visibility is “Members since invited” and the room is made accessible to anyone - the user would still need to join the room to see history, and they only see messages sent after they joined.
Secondly, it shall be noted that even after the history visibility of that room is changed to “Members (full history)”, this won’t affect past messages - those messages can still only be seen by members who were invited (or already members) at the time the message was sent.
🔗
Why would I want to make history visible to anyone?
Making the history visible to anyone means that people can see what is being said in a room before joining it. So typically, if you are browsing the room directory and see a room which might be interesting, you will have the opportunity to “peek” into it, having a view of what has been said without joining it. This gives users the opportunity to gauge a room before joining. It is a useful option for rooms publicly listed and hosting public discussions, so that they have an idea of what they’re going into before joining.
Note: for security reasons, this option is only offered when the room is accessible to anyone and the room is not end-to-end encrypted.
🔗
What is a room address?
By default the room has an ugly identifier which is barely human readable. Setting an address for a room allows you to give it a simple reference, making it easier to share a link to it. The addresses are linked to the server you are registered on (e.g. Matrix.org if your ID is @username:matrix.org). A room can have different addresses on the same homeserver and addresses on different homeservers. They are just a user friendly entry point

analysis.name: Element
analysis.value_proposition: Sovereign, interoperable and secure communications built on the Matrix open standard, self-hostable and available air-gapped for governments and regulated enterprises.
analysis.ideal_customer: European governments, defense/national security, healthcare, and regulated public-sector organizations needing digitally sovereign, self-hosted or air-gapped secure messaging as an alternative to Microsoft Teams, Slack, WhatsApp, and Signal.

features.name: end-to-end encrypted messaging
features.description: E2EE 1:1 and group chat messaging.
features.evidence_quote: End-to-end encrypted messaging and calls

features.name: end-to-end encrypted voice and video calls
features.description: E2EE voice/video calling built on MatrixRTC.
features.evidence_quote: End-to-end encrypted messaging and calls

features.name: element call
features.description: Native group video conferencing built into Element.
features.evidence_quote: Includes Element Call

features.name: federation
features.description: Cross-org/cross-server federated communication over Matrix.
features.evidence_quote: Enable or block federation.

features.name: self-hosting
features.description: Deploy the server suite on your own infrastructure.
features.evidence_quote: Self-hosted

features.name: air-gapped deployment
features.description: Install and run without internet connectivity for isolated/high-side networks.
features.evidence_quote: Air-gapped installation

features.name: decentralized architecture
features.description: No central point of failure; resilient network built on Matrix.
features.evidence_quote: With no central point of failure, Element protects against global outages

features.name: open source
features.description: Distributed under AGPL open-source license.
features.evidence_quote: Open source under AGPL

features.name: matrix protocol
features.description: Built on the Matrix open standard for interoperable real-time communications.
features.evidence_quote: built on the Matrix open standard

features.evidence_quote: Synapse Community edition homeserver

features.evidence_quote: Next generation homeserver for professional use that provides dynamic scaling

features.name: auto-scaling
features.description: Dynamically scales resources based on load.
features.evidence_quote: Dynamically scale to save resources

features.name: high availability
features.description: Automatic failover and rolling updates.
features.evidence_quote: Automatic failover and rolling updates for additional stability

features.name: s3 support
features.description: Media repository storage on S3.
features.evidence_quote: Support for media repository stored on S3.

features.evidence_quote: Official helm charts from Element. Easy Kubernetes-based installation.

features.evidence_quote: Support for OpenShift

features.description: Ready for GitOps and Continuous Deployment pipelines.
features.evidence_quote: Ready to use with GitOps processes and Continuous Deployment (CD) tooling.

features.name: sso
features.description: Single Sign-On via OIDC.
features.evidence_quote: OIDC authentication to support SSO.

features.name: ldap authentication
features.description: Authenticate users against LDAP or Active Directory.
features.evidence_quote: Authenticate users against an external LDAP or Active Directory system.

features.name: scim provisioning
features.description: Automated user management via SCIM.
features.evidence_quote: Integrate with enterprise identity management systems (LDAP and SCIM)

features.name: identity and access management
features.description: Enterprise IAM integration mirroring org structure in room hierarchies.
features.evidence_quote: Advanced Identity and Access Management

features.name: admin console
features.description: Single-pane-of-glass admin interface.
features.evidence_quote: Configure and control your deployment from a single pane of glass.

features.name: audit logs
features.description: Visible in-room auditing for compliance and record-keeping.
features.evidence_quote: Visible in-room auditing to capture an official record of discussion

features.name: moderation tools
features.description: Corporate oversight and management of conversations.
features.evidence_quote: Corporate oversight and management of your organisation’s conversations.

features.name: retention policies
features.description: Rules-based message/media retention configuration.
features.evidence_quote: Configure rules-based retention policies for messages and media

features.name: data export
features.description: Migrate your data out to avoid vendor lock-in.
features.evidence_quote: The ability to easily migrate your data, protecting against vendor lock-in.

features.name: cross-domain gateway
features.description: Hardware-based data sharing between classification boundaries.
features.evidence_quote: Hardware-based solution to enable data sharing between information classification boundaries

features.name: secure border gateway
features.description: Rules-based application firewall with federation controls.
features.evidence_quote: Rules-based application firewall with federation controls

features.name: custom mobile push gateway
features.description: Push notifications via customer's own infrastructure.
features.evidence_quote: Push notifications via your own infrastructure.

features.name: dedicated mobile push gateway

features.evidence_quote: Dedicated mobile push gateway

features.name: advance security advisory

features.name: curated security updates

features.description: 4 business hour response SLA with enhanced options.
features.evidence_quote: Guaranteed response times. 4 Business hours

features.name: training and deployment
features.description: Expert-led onboarding for admins and trainers.
features.evidence_quote: Enable your teams with expert-led onboarding.

features.evidence_quote: By default you’ll be notified if your display name and your user name are mentioned.

features.name: email invitations

features.evidence_quote: using their Matrix ID (if they have one) or by email

differentiators: Built on the Matrix open standard for vendor-agnostic federation across organizations and nations
Air-gapped, isolated, and high-side deployments with no internet connectivity required
Digital sovereignty positioning — self-hosted, open source (AGPL), no vendor lock-in
Created by the Matrix creators (Element team, 2014); largest public Matrix deployment (60M+ users)
Synapse Pro delivers 90%+ resource savings vs Community Synapse
Cross-domain hardware gateway for classified/unclassified boundary comms
Deep public-sector compliance stack (ISO 27001, Cyber Essentials Plus, CRA, WCAG AA 2.2, BITV 2.0)

praised: Open standard, sovereign, and federated — cited by European governments (Germany, Sweden, Belgium) as strategic infrastructure
Strong developer/HN attention around funding and Matrix ecosystem (809 upvotes: 'Element raises $30M to boost Matrix')
complaints: Historically flagged: 'Element (Matrix chat app) suspended from the Google Play Store' (2009 upvotes on HN) — reputational hit around app store moderation
Encrypted message search only works on desktop, not web or mobile — friction called out in official FAQs

free_tier.limits: ESS Community (AGPL), self-hosted, non-production; free frontend + backend; includes Element Call

tiers.name: Community
tiers.headline_inclusions: Free AGPL self-host, Synapse Community, Element Call — for evaluations/non-professional use

tiers.name: Enterprise (ESS Pro)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Priced per seat/month; Synapse Pro, dynamic scaling, SSO/LDAP/SCIM, compliance, SLAs (100+ users)

tiers.name: Sovereign (Air-gapped)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Priced per deployment; air-gapped install, dedicated push gateway, no internet needed

pricing.notes: Enterprise tier requires 100+ users and is priced per seat/month; Sovereign tier is priced per deployment (not per seat); all paid tiers gated behind 'Talk to an expert' sales conversation. Community edition is AGPL open source, free but explicitly not for production use.
